feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue with cross tab download in SAC.
entry.content: When exporting a table from SAP Analytics Cloud to Excel, dimensions and measures are not exported in a flat tabular structure. The business requirement is to have all dimensions and measures displayed in a single row per record in the exported file. We have reviewed table and export settings but could not achieve the expected output. Pleaselet us know if there is a configuration to enable flat exports, or if this is a known limitation/enhancement request.

entry.title: Live Data Connections from Snowflake to SAC
entry.content:
Hello, I am trying to connect SAC to Snowflake using Live data connection. I dont see snowflake under live data section(under Connections tab).  I only see option for acquiring data. We are on Version 2026.8.10 with Hana cloud version 2026.2.17(SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment Landscape type). 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Background export goes on hold when moved out of window
entry.content:
While exporting a story, by enabling background export the progress bar doesn't move if the user switches to a different window.

The exported story (PDF/PPT) doesn't show in the download section of the notification tab

entry.title: SAC Planungsmodel - Datensperre
entry.content:
I am unable to enable and save Data Lock in my SAP Analytics Cloud planning model.

Whenever I try to activate and save the Data Lock setting, the following error message appears:

Error:

Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'id')

entry.title: Running Total with Advanced Formulas
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I have a model that I want to use to train a Time Series Forecast in a Predictive Scenario. However, what I want to use for the training is not the values as they are, but the running total, because that gives a way smoother trend line which is easier for the model to forecast. I've tried first to create a Calculated Measure:

ITERATE(PRIOR() + [value], [value], [d/year_period].[p/CALMONTH])

 

(In the actual model there is also generic Accounts dimension, but for simplicity sake I omitted it from the example)

And this has the intended behaviour, but because it doesn't store the values in the model I cannot use it for the Predictive Scenario. I also tried to copy the results of the Calculated Measure into another model with a Copy Action, but that didn't work. It just copied the same values as in the "values" measure instead of the calculated running total.

My question: Do I have to do this with Advanced Formulas and if so how do I achieve this?

Thank you very much in advance and best regards

Marvin

entry.title: Filtering Out Conditions in SAC Crosstab Total
entry.content: I don't know if anyone has run into this problem, but I am creating a crosstab table, and I do not want the "Total" rows at the bottom to display the aggregated sum total (as they usually do).  I need a particular condition to be filtered out of the total only, but not the detail in the reporting rows above it.  If I apply any filter (page, table, or dimension), it filters out all the rows I need to see.  If I create restricted measures to put at the bottom,  they will appear at the bottom of each result (not the very bottom of the table).  And the result that needs to be filtered out will only show blanks or zeroes because the restricted measures filtered out their results.  I only want the totals at the bottom and not within the context of the table after every result.  If you look at the example below, I want to filter out Pack Code "0" from the totals at the bottom, so it only shows the totals for all the Pack Code "12s"; however, I need to leave Pack Code "0" in the report (just do not need those numbers coming into the totals row).

entry.title: SAC Running Total and Sorting – Is There a Workaround?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

we are facing an issue with Running Totals in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and are looking for possible workarounds or best practices.

Scenario

We have a story containing a measure that is displayed as a Running Total. As long as the table remains in its default dimension order, the cumulative values are calculated correctly.

However, as soon as a user changes the sort order of the table (e.g., Product A–Z, descending, by measure value, etc.), the Running Total no longer reflects the displayed order. The cumulative values appear incorrect from the user's perspective.

Senario from sap for me: 

 

 

Findings So Far

According to SAP KBA 3294430, this behavior is by design.

In short, the Running Total calculation is based on the underlying hierarchy or dimension order in the model rather than the sort order currently displayed in the SAC table. As a result, the displayed Running Total can become misleading once users apply their own sorting.

Attempted Workaround

As an alternative, we tried to simulate a Running Total using table formulas similar to Excel formulas, for example: =D3+B4 or =$D3+$B$4

Initially, this works as expected. However, once the user changes the table sorting, the formulas produce #REF! errors because the cell references are no longer valid.

Question

Has anyone found a reliable workaround to calculate a Running Total that follows the current sort order displayed in the SAC table?

Alternatively, is there a way to implement such a calculation using SAC table formulas, calculated measures, scripting, or another approach that remains stable even when users change the sorting?

Any ideas, recommendations, or experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Using an generic parameter in Multi Action
entry.content:
Hi team,

I am currently working on a Multi Action in SAP Analytics Cloud that contains four Data Actions from different models.

Is there a way to create a generic parameter (for example, a Year parameter) so that I only need to enter a value such as 2028 once, and then have all the Data Actions within the Multi Action automatically use that same year value?

In other words, I would like to maintain a single parameter at the Multi Action level and pass it to all underlying Data Actions, avoiding the need to enter the year separately for each one.

Has anyone implemented something similar or can confirm whether this is supported?

Muti action:

Data actions in question with year:

 

 

 

Thanks in advance.
Ganjos

entry.title: Best practices on self service roles
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have an issue with security roles. 
Introduction: With my team we are starting a self-service project where users will log into sap analytics cloud and can create their own stories.
Context: we have the "scoped user" role assig to these users, with this role they can see data from models in the "QA" and "PRD" spaces,  because they need access to them to be able to see any data on the stories developed by the data engineers.
issue: With this self-service project, this role that I mentioned before (scoped user) lets them see and use all the models in the "QA" and "PRD" spaces in sap analytics cloud, and we only want them to be able to access the models on their own space (EG: If the user is from human resources area, they will only have access to the Human Resources space in datasphere for self service).

Is the issue explained clearly enough?

have a nice week.

entry.title: 403 Forbidden Error while using SAP SAC Data Import Service API
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am trying to push data into a SAC model using Data Import Service API. I am consistently getting a 403 Forbidden error while creating the import job, even though authentication and model configuration seem correct.
SAP User Role: Admin
Model Permission: Data Import API is enabled

OAuth Client Configuration

Purpose: API Access

Access: Data Import Service

Grant Type: Client Credentials

Token generation works successfully

entry.title: 403 Forbidden Error while using SAP SAC Data Import Service API (Third party application)
entry.content:
I am trying to integrate Microsoft Fabric (Notebook/Pipeline) with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using the Data Import Service API to push data into a SAC model.

However, I am consistently getting a 403 Forbidden error while creating the import job, even though authentication and model configuration seem correct.

Tenant: eu20.analytics.cloud.sap
Model Type: Planning Model
Model Setting:  Allow Import via Data Import API enabled
User Role: Admin

OAuth Client Configuration

Purpose: API Access
Access:Data Import Service
Grant Type: Client Credentials
Token generation works successfully

entry.title: Custom Widgets in SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

Does SAC support Custom Widgets in stories that consume live models from SAP Datasphere? Are there any specific limitations or configuration requirements for this setup?

entry.title: configuration of custom Email notification from SAC using Microsoft Entra app is not successful
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We were using default option from SAC to send email notifications. We followed below Community URL blog to achieve this configuration. Please find attached the configuration setting screenshot from SAC and the har files generated while trying to establish this connection with Microsoft'a Entra.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/steps-to-configure-custom-smtp-server-on-sap-analytics-cloud/ba-p/13579091

 Regards

Shafi

entry.title: SAC data action: growth forecast from 3-year average returns 0 for rounded value
entry.content:
I’m working on an SAP Analytics Cloud data action for headcount forecasting and I’m facing an issue with rounding and aggregation.

Requirement:

  • User enters a growth % in an input field
  • The script calculates the average of the last 3 years of actuals
  • That average is multiplied by (growth %)
  • The result is copied to the next forecast year

My issue:

  • When I use ROUND(. . . , 0) , the result becomes 0 
  • When I use ROUND(. . . , 2), the result shows 3.60 instead of 4

I know the calculation to be happening at the leaf date/month level, while the story is displaying a year total, so the result is being aggregated rather than written as a single yearly value.

How can I make SAC write the forecast as a single rounded yearly value instead of aggregating monthly/leaf values?

Here is the data action logic i used: 

/*
Generate growth headcount forecast
*/
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Amount"
MEMBERSET [d/Data_Source] = "DS01"
MEMBERSET [d/Employee_Type] = "FTE"
MEMBERSET [d/Entity] = "E01"
MEMBERSET [d/Cost_Center] = "10"
MEMBERSET [d/Account] = "S006"
MEMBERSET [d/Position] = "AAP_11"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = [d/Version].[p/Start_Date] TO NEXT(1,"YEAR",[d/Version].[p/End_Date])

FLOAT @Growth
FLOAT @AvgHist = 0.0
FLOAT @AVG= 0.0
FLOAT @Y1= 0.0
FLOAT @Y2 = 0.0
FLOAT @Y3= 0.0

@Growth = %P_GROWTH% / 100

//FOREACH [d/Date]
@Y1= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Actual",[d/Date] = PREVIOUS(1,"YEAR",[d/Version].[p/Start_Date]))
@Y2 = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Actual",[d/Date] = PREVIOUS(2,"YEAR",[d/Version].[p/Start_Date]))
@Y3= RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Actual",[d/Date] = PREVIOUS(3,"YEAR",[d/Version].[p/Start_Date]))

@AvgHist = (@AvgHist+ (@Y1+@Y2+@Y3))

//@AvgHist = @AvgHist+RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Actual",[d/Date] = PREVIOUS(3,"YEAR",[d/Version].[p/Start_Date]))

@AVG= @AvgHist/3
DATA () = ROUND(@Avg *(1+@Growth), 0)
//ENDFOR

-- Updating the post - Issue has been fixed,  updated in styling to set decimal as 0.

Thanks,

Vinay

entry.title: Fill Rate Cannot Be Calculated at Header Level Using Alternate Calculation Field
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are trying to calculate the Fill Rate % at the Sales Order Header level in an SAP Custom Analytical Query.

Requirement:
Fill Rate % = (Total Invoiced Quantity ÷ Total Ordered Quantity) × 100

Example:

Order Quantity = 13,600 PC
Invoiced Quantity = 9,051 PC
Expected Fill Rate % = 66.55%
We attempted to create this using an Alternate Calculation Field in the Custom Analytical Query, but the system calculates the percentage at the item level and does not perform the calculation based on the aggregated header quantities. Functions such as aggregation and division on the header totals are not producing the expected result.

Questions:

Is it possible to calculate Fill Rate % on the header level using an Alternate Calculation Field in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
Is there any standard approach, CDS view enhancement, or analytical modeling technique to achieve this requirement?
If not possible in standard functionality, would this require a custom development approach (for example, BTP development)?
Any guidance or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: How to connect to INFORMATICA through Rest API in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We want to connect to INFORMATICA data in SAC. As far as I know we can connect to INFORMATICA data in SAC either through Open Connector or through REST API. Can someone please help on how to connect through Rest API?

If the Informatica developer provides an API  can I consume in SAC ? 

Thanks in advance.

Regards

Ahmed Baseer

entry.title: Where to download BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 2025?
entry.content:
Hello,

We have currently installed the latest version of SAP BO 2025 on a test server.

Now I would like to establish a connection from the SAC to the BO and am looking for the latest BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 2025.

This LDC is already referenced in the following guide:
https://help.sap.com/doc/8238f7ab22884808a738768cdebe30b9/latest/en-US/sbo_live_data_connect_user_3.0.pdf

Or does anyone have experience with version 4.3.3 and BO 2025?

Thanks for your help!

entry.title: BI Report Development for Job Families (Employee Central) – Data Modelling and Reporting Approach
entry.content:
In a Webi crosstab report or a SAC dashboard, when using the following dimensions:

  • Job Family
  • Job Sub Family
  • Job Code

The Job Codes are not correctly aligned to their respective Job Sub Families.

Observed Behaviour:

  • Job Codes appear duplicated across multiple Job Sub Families
  • Job Codes that should belong to a specific Sub Family are shown under incorrect Sub Families

E.g Job Family set to Finance:

entry.title: export destination not showed up
entry.content:
Export destination is not showed up  please see attached

 

New Export - Export - SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Copy-paste error when time hierarchy differs between input control and table
entry.content:
When a number is entered into this story FDT_FINANCE_OVW_0001, it cannot be copied to the rest of the year because of the below error message. Previously it wasn't a problem.Error Message:Query based copy is not supported for this combination of source and target cells : Different hierarchies are used for Time.CALMONTH
Correlation ID: 13900068242346038257121872538852
